title: New States Global Resistance and the End of the Cold War
description: This exercise will model items that cover the political and economic development of new states in the years following World War II, global resistance to Cold War power structures, and the end of the Cold War. Each model will scale 3 items, for a total of 12.
topic_path: The End of the Cold War | 8.8
New States Global Resistance and the End of the Cold War

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best identifies a direct cause of the creation of new states after World War II?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rise of nationalism in former colonies
the emergence of communist ideas
the intensification of imperial conquest
economic development in Western Europe created a need for more raw materials

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following examples might a historian use to argue that religion complicated the foundation of new states in the latter half of the twentieth century?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Muslims feared marginalization under a Hindu-majority government.
Christian nationalism in the United States declined.
Communist China suppressed and controlled religious practices.
Traditional African belief systems grew in number and influence.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>The whole Vietnamese people, animated by a common purpose, are determined to fight to the bitter end against any attempt by the French colonialists to reconquer their country. We are convinced that the Allied nations which at Tehran and San Francisco have acknowledged the principles of self-determination and equality of nations, will not refuse to acknowledge the independence of Vietnam.
>
>A people who have courageously opposed French domination for more than eight years, a people who have fought side by side with the Allies against the Fascists during these last years, such a people must be free and independent. 
>
>For these reasons, we, members of the Provisional Government of the Democratic Republic of Vietnam, solemnly declare to the world that Vietnam has the right to be a free and independent country—and in fact is so already. The entire Vietnamese people are determined to mobilize all their physical and mental strength, to sacrifice their lives and property in order to safeguard their independence and liberty.

Source: Ho Chi Mihn, "Declaration of Independence of the Democratic Republic of Viet-Nam," 1945. 

**Which of the following best illustrates an impact of the developments described in the excerpt above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Other nations living under French colonial rule were inspired to use guerilla warfare to achieve independence.
The Soviet Union reformed its policies to include more political freedoms for its citizens.
The independence struggle in India incorporated tactics from Vietnam.
Communism spread across Asia, creating a crisis for the Soviets in Afghanistan.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>For centuries, Europeans dominated the African continent. The white man arrogated to himself the right to rule and to be obeyed by the non-white; his mission, he claimed, was to “civilize” Africa. Under this cloak, the Europeans robbed the continent of vast riches and inflicted unimaginable suffering on the African people.
>
>All this makes a sad story, but now we must be prepared to bury the past with its unpleasant memories and look to the future. All we ask of the former colonial powers is their goodwill and co-operation to remedy past mistakes and injustices and to grant independence to the colonies in Africa?
>
>It is clear that we must find an African solution to our problems, and that this can only be found in African unity. Divided we are weak; united, Africa could become one of the greatest forces for good in the world.

Source: Kwame Nkrumah, “I Speak of Freedom: A Statement of African Ideology,” 1961, Ghana. 

**The ideas expressed in the excerpt above most strongly support which of the following statements about decolonization after World War II?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Ghana helped other African nations achieve independence.
Revolutions led to the rise of military regimes in some decolonizing nations.
Communist revolutions spread throughout the continent of Africa, bolstered by the Soviet Union.
Neocolonialism replaced traditional colonial empires.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>...A true revolution of values will soon look uneasily on the glaring contrast of poverty and wealth. With righteous indignation, it will look across the seas and see individual capitalists of the West investing huge sums of money in Asia, Africa and South America, only to take the profits out with no concern for the social betterment of the countries, and say: “This is not just.” It will look at our alliance with the landed gentry of Latin America and say: “This is not just.” 
>
>...A true revolution of values will lay hands on the world order and say of war: “This way of settling differences is not just.” This business of burning human beings with napalm, of filling our nation’s homes with orphans and widows, of injecting poisonous drugs of hate into veins of people normally humane, of sending men home from dark and bloody battlefields physically handicapped and psychologically deranged, cannot be reconciled with wisdom, justice and love.  
>
>A nation that continues year after year to spend more money on military defense than on programs of social uplift is approaching spiritual death.

Source: Dr Martin Luther King, Jr.,  “Beyond Vietnam.” Speech, New York City, April 4, 1967. 

**The views expressed in the excerpt above are best seen as evidence of which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The American Civil Rights movement and global decolonization movements mutually supported and influenced each other.
Martin Luther King Jr. was a proponent of communism in the United States and abroad.
Martin Luther King Jr. supported pan-Africanism as the only way forward for Africans and African Americans.
Many non-violent and pacifist political and civil movements in the twentieth century succeeded.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>As your Government did not respond to our demands, the All-In African National Council ... called for a General Strike on the 29th, 30th and 31st of last month. As predicted in my letter of 20 April 1961, your Government sought to suppress the strike by force. You rushed a special law in Parliament authorizing the detention without trial of people connected with the organization of the strike. The army was mobilized and European civilians armed. More than ten thousand innocent Africans were arrested under the pass laws and meetings banned throughout the country.

>…For our own part, we wish to make it perfectly clear that we shall never cease to fight against repression and injustice, and we are resuming active opposition against your regime. In taking this decision we must again stress that we have no illusions of the serious implications of our decision.

>We know that your Government will once again unleash all its fury and barbarity to persecute the African people. But as the result of the last strike has proved, no power on earth can stop an oppressed people, determined to win their freedom. History punishes those who resort to force and fraud to suppress the claims and legitimate aspirations of the majority of the country’s citizens.

Source: Nelson Mandela, Second Letter from Nelson Mandela to Hendrik Verwoerd, June 26, 1961. OER

**Which of the following is most similar to the actions described in the excerpt above?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Mahatma Gandhi’s fight to liberate India
Ho Chi Mihn’s campaign to free Vietnam
Al-Qaeda's campaign against American imperialism
Algerian nationalists fighting against French rule

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Irish Republican Army best serves as an example of which of the following?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: violent resistance
pacifist activism
protestant ideals
spread of communism

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the kinds of actions taken by Shining Path in Peru in the 1980s and 1990s to achieve a communist revolution?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: terrorism
strikes
mobilizing industrial workers
elections

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following developments eventually contributed to the fall of the Soviet Union in the late 20th century?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Soviet leaders allowed groups to retain some aspects of their ethnic identities but maintained strict central control.
Soviet satellite states entered into the Warsaw Pact.
Soviet officials clamped down on freedom of information and expression.
The Soviet economy became increasingly centralized, restricting private ownership and free markets.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best illustrates the long-term impact of Mikhail Gorbachev’s policies of *Glasnost* and *Perestroika*?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Increased civil liberties led to widespread protests and open rebellion against the Soviet Union.
The economy of the Soviet Union improved with new trade agreements from international businesses.
Many ethnic and nationalist movements across the USSR were harshly repressed.
Social and political reforms ushered in a period of stability following a period of crisis.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following provides the broad historical context for the fall of the Soviet Union?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rise of nationalist movements around the world
total war during World War I and II
the rise of empires
the formation of the European Union

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes a contradiction of the Soviet Union’s policies in the latter half of the twentieth century?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They lent support for revolutions across the world while brutally repressing their own people.
They allowed for limited market reforms within the Soviet Union but rejected capitalism in their satellite states.
They permitted groups to maintain their ethnic identities but forced everyone to speak Russian.
They promoted communist ideals but fell short of achieving them.
